zh.lifeSummary: Madog ap Hywel Graunt de Turberville是Alys verch Llewelyn的兒子，於1335年出生在威爾士蒙姆爾阿加文尼。他於1355年與Catherine verch Merig Llwyd在威爾士蒙姆爾阿加文尼結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1396年在他的家鄉享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Madog ap Hywel Graunt de Turberville是Alys verch Llewelyn的儿子，于1335年出生在威尔士蒙姆爾阿加文尼。他于1355年与Catherine verch Merig Llwyd在威尔士蒙姆爾阿加文尼结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1396年在他的家乡享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
meanings: A Welsh name. Some write the name Hoel, which they render 'lordly;' if so, it may come from huail, a vice-regent, regent. Arthur derives the surname Howell from Corn. -Brit, houl, the sun; and he adds, high, exalted.' But Hywel is rather from hywel, conspicuous.

nameMeanings.name: Graunt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish, English, and especially Scottish (of Norman origin): nickname from Anglo-Norman French graund, graunt ‘tall, large’ (Old French grand, grant, from Latin grandis), given either to a person of remarkable size, or else in a relative way to distinguish two bearers of the same personal name, often representatives of different generations within the same family.
English: from the rare Middle English (and Old English) personal name Grante or Grente.
Irish: in Ireland this is usually the Norman Scottish name (see 1 above), but it was also adopted for Irish Mag Raighne, see Graney .

variants.name: Graney
meanings: Irish (Galway, Cork, and Kerry): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mag Raighne ‘son of Raighne’, a pet form of Raghnall. It has sometimes been changed to Grant . See also Rainey .
Possibly an altered form of Swiss German Gräni, unexplained.

variants.name: Grand
meanings: French: nickname from Old French grand, grant ‘tall, large’, from Latin grandis. See also Grant 1 and 4, compare Degrand and Legrand .
German: topographic name for someone who lived in a gravelly area, from Middle Low German grand ‘coarse gravel’.
German: nickname from a southern word of uncertain origin meaning ‘frustration, anger, ire’.

variants.name: Grande
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Rocco, Domenic, Mario, Antonio, Santo, Orlando, Americo, Carmine, Gennaro, Luigi, Pasquale, Saverio, Alberico.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Alicia, Ricardo, Jorge, Juan, Raul, Angel, Atilio.
Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: nickname for someone of large stature, in either a literal or figurative sense, from grande ‘tall, large’.
German: habitational name from Grande in Holstein or Grand in Bavaria.

variants.name: General
meanings: African American: from the English vocabulary word general, possibly adopted as a surname in honor of a certain general of the US army, most probably Ulysses S. Grant (see Grant , compare President ). Compare also 3 below.
German: nickname for a mercenary (employed in royal services).
Hispanic (mainly Philippines): nickname from Spanish general ‘general’ (military officer).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Turberville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name, probably from Thouberville (Eure), or possibly from Trubleville (Seine-Maritime), both in France. The two may have been confused in surname usage.

variants.name: Torbet
meanings: Scottish:

from the Anglo-Norman and Middle English personal name T(h)urbert, T(h)orbert, a hybrid of Old Norse Thorr ‘Thor’, the name of the god of thunder, + ancient Germanic -berht ‘bright, famous’. The name was probably formed on the Continent; see Turberville .
perhaps in some cases a nickname from Old French and Middle English turbot, turbut ‘turbot’, for someone who caught or sold or resembled this fish.

variants.name: Turbyfill
meanings: Altered form of English   Turberville  .

variants.name: Tuberville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Turberville  . This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Turville
meanings: English (Surrey and Hampshire; of Norman origin):
habitational name from Tourville‐la‐Campagne (Eure, France). The placename derives from the Old Norse personal name Thórr + Old French ville ‘farmstead, estate’ (from Latin villa).
occasionally a shortened form of   Turberville  .

variants.name: Turbeville
meanings: English: variant of   Turberville  .

variants.name: Troublefield
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Turberville  .

nameMeanings.name: Madog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name, possibly having the sense ‘fortunate’ or alternatively from Celtic aodh ‘fire’.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: Madog ap Hywel Graunt de Turberville was born in 1335, in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales as the son of Alys verch Llewelyn. He married Catherine verch Merig Llwyd in 1355, in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1396, in his hometown, at the age of 61.

fr.Llanfaiscilgedin, Abergavenny, Monmouths: Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Madog ap Hywel Graunt de Turberville est né en 1335, à Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles, fils de Alys verch Llewelyn. Il a épousé Catherine verch Merig Llwyd en 1355, à Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1396, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Madog ap Hywel Graunt de Turberville wurde 1335 in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales, als Sohn von Alys verch Llewelyn geboren. Er hat 1355 in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales Catherine verch Merig Llwyd geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1396 im Alter von 61 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Madog ap Hywel Graunt de Turberville è nato il nel 1335 nel Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles come figlio di Alys verch Llewelyn. Lui sposato Catherine verch Merig Llwyd nel 1355, nel Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1396, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Madog ap Hywel Graunt de Turbervilleは1335年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアアバーガベニーでAlys verch Llewelynの息子として生まれた。1355年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアアバーガベニーでCatherine verch Merig Llwydと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1396年に故郷で61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Madog ap Hywel Graunt de Turberville(은)는 1335년 웨일스 몬모우스실르 압르가븐이에서 Alys verch Llewelyn의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1355년 웨일스 몬모우스실르 압르가븐이에서 Catherine verch Merig Llwyd(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1396년 그의 고향에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Madog ap Hywel Graunt de Turberville nasceu em 1335, em Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Gales filho de Alys verch Llewelyn. Ele casou-se com Catherine verch Merig Llwyd em 1355, em Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1396, em sua cidade natal, com 61 anos.

ro.Llanfaiscilgedin, Abergavenny, Monmouths: Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Țara Galilor, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Madog ap Hywel Graunt de Turberville родился в 1335 г. в местности абиргавенни, монмоутшир, Уэльс. Его мать звали Alys verch Llewelyn. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine verch Merig Llwyd в 1355 г. в местности абиргавенни, монмоутшир, Уэльс. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1396 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Madog ap Hywel Graunt de Turberville nació en 1335, en Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Gales como hijo de Alys verch Llewelyn. Se casó con Catherine verch Merig Llwyd en 1355, en Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1396, en su pueblo, a la edad de 61 años.
